title: Introduction to Python on the Pico
description: Learn to program the Raspberry Pi Pico with MicroPython — from wiring your first LED to controlling NeoPixel strips, DC motors, and servo motors.

labs.title: Lab 1: Getting Started
labs.desc: Install MicroPython on the Pico and run a Hello World program

labs.title: Lab 2: LED Test
labs.desc: Build a simple LED circuit and prove the hardware works

labs.title: Lab 3: LED Control
labs.desc: Control an LED from the MicroPython console using GPIO

labs.title: Lab 1: Running Code
labs.desc: Put a program inside the Pico which runs when it is switched on

labs.title: Lab 2: LED Flashing
labs.desc: Write a program that flashes an LED using while True and time.sleep

labs.title: Lab 3: Switch Input
labs.desc: Read a push button from the console and understand pull-up resistors

labs.title: Lab 4: Switch and LED
labs.desc: Write a program that lights an LED when a button is pressed

labs.title: Lab 1: Pixel Test
labs.desc: Set up a NeoPixel strand from the console and explore colour

labs.title: Lab 2: Colour Range
labs.desc: Write a program that displays a spread of colours across the strip

labs.title: Lab 3: Palette and Button
labs.desc: Define colour palettes and use a button to cycle between them

labs.title: Lab 4: Lerp Fade
labs.desc: Use linear interpolation to smoothly fade between colours

labs.title: Lab 5: Non-Blocking Animation
labs.desc: Use Python generators to run multiple animations with button switching

labs.title: Lab 1: Software PWM
labs.desc: Build a PWM signal in software and understand duty cycle

labs.title: Lab 2: Hardware PWM
labs.desc: Use the Pico's built-in PWM hardware for precise waveforms

labs.title: Lab 3: L298N H-Bridge
labs.desc: Connect a DC motor to the L298N and control direction and speed

labs.title: Lab 4: Motor Driver Class
labs.desc: Wrap the L298N control logic in a clean Python class

labs.title: Lab 5: Button-Controlled Motor
labs.desc: Use buttons to cycle motor speed and toggle direction

labs.title: Lab 1: Servo PWM
labs.desc: Understand the 50 Hz PWM signal that controls servo position

labs.title: Lab 2: I2C Introduction
labs.desc: Learn the I2C protocol and scan for devices on the bus

labs.title: Lab 3: PCA9685 Registers
labs.desc: Write directly to PCA9685 registers to move a servo

labs.title: Lab 4: Servo Driver Class
labs.desc: Build PCA9685 and ServoDriver classes with a clean angle() interface

labs.title: Lab 5: Multi-Servo Control
labs.desc: Control four servos independently using four buttons
